 {"id":"gt-48bs","title":"gt rig reset: clear stale mail on reset/land","description":"## Problem

When resetting or landing a rig/town, stale mail messages can confuse agents on startup. Old handoff messages, daemon notifications, and inter-agent mail should be cleaned up as part of reset.

## Current State

- `gt rig reset` exists but doesn't clear mail
- Stale messages accumulate (e.g., daemon SHUTDOWN messages)
- Agents may read outdated context on startup

## Proposed Behavior

`gt rig reset` and `gt town reset` should:
1. Close all open messages (`--type=message`) in the relevant beads
2. Optionally preserve pinned handoff beads (clear content, keep bead)
3. Log what was cleaned up

```bash
gt rig reset gastown          # Clears gastown mail
gt rig reset gastown --mail   # Only clear mail, keep other state
gt town reset                 # Clears all town-level mail
gt town reset --all           # Clears mail in all rigs too
```

## Implementation

1. Query `bd list --type=message --status=open`
2. Close each with reason 'Cleared during reset'
3. For pinned handoffs: `bd update <id> --description=''` instead of close","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T11:42:17.769674-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:27:49.532203-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:27:49.532203-08:00","close_reason":"Added --mail flag to gt rig reset"}

 {"id":"gt-4eim","title":"gt nudge should accept flexible session identifiers","description":"Currently `gt nudge` requires the exact tmux session name (e.g., `gt-gastown-furiosa`).

It should also accept:
- `gastown/furiosa` (rig/polecat format)
- `furiosa` (polecat name, infer rig from cwd or require if ambiguous)

The session list command shows `gastown/furiosa` format, but nudge rejects it:
```
gt session list → shows 'gastown/furiosa'
gt nudge gastown/furiosa 'msg' → 'session not found'
gt nudge gt-gastown-furiosa 'msg' → works
```

Should normalize all these formats to the tmux session name internally.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T15:36:45.013475-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:30:11.942309-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-4ev4","title":"Implement gt sling command","description":"The unified work dispatch command.

```bash
gt sling <thing> <target> [options]
```

Implements spawn + assign + pin in one operation. See sling-design.md.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Parse thing (proto name, issue ID, epic ID)
- [ ] Parse target (agent address)  
- [ ] Spawn molecule if proto
- [ ] Assign to target agent
- [ ] Pin to agent's hook (pinned bead)
- [ ] Support --wisp flag for ephemeral
- [ ] Support --molecule flag for issue+workflow
- [ ] Error if hook already occupied (unless --force)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/furiosa","created_at":"2025-12-22T03:17:27.273013-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T12:33:43.076291-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T12:33:43.076291-08:00","close_reason":"Closed"}

 {"id":"gt-975","title":"Molecule execution support for polecats and crew","description":"Enable agents to run bonded molecules through the full lifecycle.

## The Model
1. BOND - bd mol bond <template> --assignee <identity>
   Creates concrete issues, assigns root to agent

2. DISCOVER - Agent finds assigned molecules via bd ready
   DAG structure shows unblocked steps

3. WORK - Agent works through DAG, closing steps as done
   Can delegate children to lower-tier agents (haiku)

4. SURVIVE - Agent dies → beads persist
   Any agent resumes from DAG state

5. SUPERVISE - Witness monitors for stalled molecules
   Nudges owner or reassigns if dead

## Questions to Resolve
- Seed node terminology: seed vs root vs pole vs nucleus
- Assignee inheritance: single owner vs delegation model
- Stall detection: heartbeat vs activity timeout vs session monitoring

## Implementation
- Polecat startup: check for assigned molecules
- Polecat work loop: follow molecule DAG
- Witness: monitor molecule progress, detect stalls
- Mayor: can assign molecules, handle escalations

## Depends On
- beads: bd mol bond command (bd-usro in beads repo)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T16:57:01.09104-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:02:25.133021-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T12:02:25.133021-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented molecule execution support for polecats:

1. spawn.go: Added MoleculeContext to work assignment mail with molecule workflow instructions
2. prime.go: Added outputMoleculeContext() that detects molecule steps and shows progress
3. molecule.go: Added 'gt molecule progress' command for Witness monitoring

Polecats now:
- Receive molecule-aware work assignments showing step N/M and workflow
- See molecule context in gt prime output when working on molecule steps
- Can follow the DAG by running 'bd ready --parent <root>' after each step

Witnesses/Mayor can:
- Monitor molecule progress with 'gt molecule progress <root-id>'
- See completion percentage, ready/blocked steps"}
 {"id":"gt-976","title":"Add crew lifecycle support to Deacon","description":"Currently crew sessions (like gastown/crew/max, beads/crew/dave) are marked as 'human-managed' and excluded from automated lifecycle.

## Current State
- `getManager(RoleCrew)` returns `"human"` (handoff.go:273)
- `gt handoff` for crew just prints a message, no lifecycle request sent
- Crew cannot request automated refresh from Deacon

## Desired State
Crew members should be able to request lifecycle actions from Deacon:
- `gt handoff --cycle` sends request to Deacon, which kills/restarts the crew session
- Useful when: context full, running molecules that need fresh session, automation

## Implementation
1. Change `getManager(RoleCrew)` to return `"deacon/"` (or new crew manager)
2. Teach Deacon to recognize crew session naming pattern
3. Add crew to Deacon's respawn loop handling
4. Test with `gt nudge` for reliable message delivery

## Use Case
Running a molecule (e.g., version-bump) and discovering mid-workflow that a fresh session is needed. Agent should be able to request refresh automatically rather than requiring human intervention.

## Related
- gt nudge: reliable message delivery to Claude sessions
- bd mol bond: molecule instantiation (coming in beads)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:19:46.854829-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:19:46.854829-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-977","title":"Work request: gt-976 crew lifecycle support","description":"Hey Max, filed gt-976 for you - adding crew lifecycle support to Deacon.

Currently crew is 'human-managed' and can't request automated refresh. Would be useful for molecules that need fresh sessions mid-workflow.

Key changes:
- getManager(RoleCrew) → return deacon/ instead of human
- Teach Deacon crew session patterns
- Test with gt nudge

Priority 2, no rush but would unblock molecule automation for crew.

- Dave (beads/crew/dave)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:19:55.964718-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:19:55.964718-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-e9k","title":"Workspace cleanup: preflight and postflight","description":"Workspace preflight and postflight commands for clean state management.

## Preflight
```
gt preflight [--rig <rig>] [--dry-run]
```

Run before starting batch work:
1. Clean stale mail in inboxes
2. Check for stuck workers (warn)
3. Check rig health (polecats, refinery)
4. Verify git state is clean
5. Run bd sync to ensure beads current

## Postflight
```
gt postflight [--rig <rig>] [--archive-mail] [--dry-run]
```

Run after batch work completes:
1. Archive old mail with --archive-mail
2. Clean up stale integration branches
3. Sync beads
4. Report on rig state

## Implementation
```go
func Preflight(rigName string, dryRun bool) (*PreflightReport, error)
func Postflight(rigName string, opts PostflightOptions) (*PostflightReport, error)
```

## Report Structures
```go
type PreflightReport struct {
    MailCleaned    int
    RigHealthy     bool
    StuckWorkers   []string
    Warnings       []string
}

type PostflightReport struct {
    MailArchived   int
    BranchesCleaned int
    Warnings       []string
}
```

## Note

These are workspace maintenance commands, not tied to "swarm" lifecycle. Run them anytime to keep the rig clean.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Preflight cleans stale state
- [ ] Postflight archives old mail
- [ ] Both have --dry-run mode
- [ ] Clear reports of actions taken","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:47:15.997677-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T17:25:01.55269-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-ebl","title":"CLI: names commands for polecat naming pool","description":"Polecat naming pool for auto-generated names.

## Commands

### gt names generate
```
gt names generate [--count N]
```
Generate N names from pool.

### gt names add
```
gt names add <name>...
```
Add custom names to pool.

### gt names list
```
gt names list
```
Show available and used names.

### gt names reset
```
gt names reset [--keep-used]
```
Reset pool to defaults.

## Config File
<rig>/town/naming.json:
```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "auto_refill": true,
  "refill_threshold": 5,
  "pool": {
    "available": ["Toast", "Nux", "Capable", ...],
    "used": ["Alice", "Bob"]
  }
}
```

## Default Pool
Mad Max themed: Toast, Nux, Capable, Furiosa, Immortan, etc.

## Integration
gt polecat add calls naming pool if no name given:
```go
if name == "" {
    name, err = naming.Generate(rigPath)
}
```

## New Package
internal/naming/
├── pool.go     # Pool management
└── defaults.go # Default name lists

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Auto-generate names on polecat add
- [ ] Track used vs available
- [ ] Auto-refill when low
- [ ] Custom names addable","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:48:33.592129-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:07:13.882465-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-ng6g","title":"Implement await type handlers (gh:run, gh:pr, timer, human, mail)","description":"Implement condition checking for each await type.

- gh:pr:<id> - Check PR merged/closed status via gh CLI  
- timer:<duration> - Simple elapsed time check
- human:<prompt> - Check for human approval (via mail?)
- mail:<pattern> - Check for mail matching pattern

## Interface
```go
type AwaitHandler interface {

}
```

 {"id":"gt-ngpz","title":"mol-christmas-launch: 3-day execution plan","description":"

---

## Wisp Integration Wave Plan (added 2025-12-21)

Dependency-ordered execution for gt-3x0z (Wisp Molecules) + gt-rana (Patrol System):

### Wave 1 (parallel - no blockers)
- gt-3x0z.1: gt rig init creates .beads-ephemeral/
- gt-3x0z.2: Configure bd for ephemeral molecule bonding
- gt-3x0z.3: gt doctor checks for ephemeral health
- gt-rana.1: Attachment field on pinned beads
- gt-rana.3: mol-deacon-patrol definition

### Wave 2 (after Wave 1)
- gt-3x0z.4: gt spawn --molecule bonds ephemeral (GATE 1)
- gt-rana.2: Daemon attachment detection

### Wave 3-5 (sequential)
- gt-3x0z.5, gt-3x0z.6 → gt-3x0z.7 → gt-3x0z.8 (GATE 2: squash)

### Wave 6+ (patrol integration)
- gt-rana.4: Basic patrol runner (needs gt-rana.3 + gt-3x0z.8)
- gt-3x0z.9: mol-deacon-patrol uses wisp (needs gt-rana.3 + gt-3x0z.8)
- Then: gt-rana.5-7, gt-3x0z.10-12

### Key Gates
1. gt-3x0z.4 - spawn/bond unlocks Phase 2
2. gt-3x0z.8 - squash unlocks patrol integration
3. gt-rana.4 - patrol runner unlocks Phase 2+ patrol

 {"id":"gt-ngu1","title":"Pinned beads should appear first in mail inbox","description":"Currently bd mail inbox sorts by priority then date, but pinned beads (handoff context) should always appear at the top.

- This enables handoff beads to be the default first item an agent sees

1. In mail.go runMailInbox(), after filtering, sort pinned beads to top
2. Within pinned and non-pinned groups, maintain priority/date sort

**Related:**
- gt-r8ej: Implement pinned beads for handoff state
- gt-8h4: Pinned Beads epic

- bd mail inbox shows pinned beads first
- Pinned beads still sorted by priority/date among themselves
- Non-pinned mail sorted normally after pinned section","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:24.133521-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T18:03:04.871622-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T18:03:04.871622-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-ngu1","depends_on_id":"gt-8h4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:45:31.978176-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-sult","title":"gt spawn beads sync warning is misleading for redirect-based polecats","description":"## Problem

When spawning a polecat, gt spawn shows a warning:
```
Warning: beads sync: exit status 1
```

This is misleading because polecats using the redirect architecture (`.beads/redirect`) share the canonical database at `mayor/rig/.beads/beads.db`. The 'stale beads' indicated by the warning is just git branch divergence (main vs beads-sync), not actual data staleness.

## Expected Behavior

gt spawn should either:
1. Skip the beads sync check for polecats using redirects (they share the canonical DB)
2. Or provide a clearer message like 'beads redirect active, using shared database'

## Reproduction

```bash
gt spawn --issue gt-xxx --rig gastown --create
# Shows 'Warning: beads sync: exit status 1' even though beads are current
```

## Root Cause

spawn.go calls beads sync and treats any non-zero exit as a warning. But with redirects, the polecat doesn't need its own beads - it uses the canonical source via the redirect chain:
```
polecat/.beads/redirect -> ../../.beads -> gastown/.beads/redirect -> mayor/rig/.beads
```

## Fix Options

1. Check for .beads/redirect before calling sync
2. Have bd sync return 0 when redirect is present
3. Suppress the warning in spawn when redirect exists","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:02:01.28061-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:02:01.28061-08:00"}

- gt mq process: Engineer processes the queue
- gt mq reorder <id> --after <x>: Change ordering via deps
- gt mq status <id>: Show MR details

All commands should work with the Beads data plane.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:16.649648-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:22:54.684284-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T20:22:54.684284-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-svi","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:55.456462-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-svi","depends_on_id":"gt-kp2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:03:12.689547-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-svi.1","title":"gt mq submit: create MR from current branch","description":"Implement 'gt mq submit' command that creates a merge-request bead.

Auto-detection logic:
1. Branch: current git branch
2. Issue: parse from branch name (polecat/Nux/gt-xyz → gt-xyz)
3. Target: main (or integration branch if --epic specified)
4. Worker: parse from branch name
5. Rig: current rig

Options:
- --branch BRANCH: explicit source branch
- --issue ISSUE: explicit source issue
- --epic EPIC: target integration/EPIC instead of main
- --priority P: override priority (default: inherit from source issue)

Creates merge-request bead and prints MR ID.

 {"id":"gt-w3bu","title":"gt spawn: Enter key needs debounce delay after paste","description":"## Problem

When spawning polecats via `gt spawn`, instructions are pasted into tmux but workers often sit idle at prompt because the Enter key arrives before the paste is fully processed.

## Observed Behavior

- Instructions pasted via tmux send-keys
- Enter key sent immediately after
- Worker session shows prompt with no input (paste not submitted)
- Requires manual intervention to nudge workers

## Expected Behavior

- Paste completes fully
- Enter key submits the pasted instructions
- Worker begins executing immediately

## Root Cause

Likely a race condition between paste buffer processing and keypress handling. Need either:
1. Debounce delay before sending Enter
2. Longer delay (Tmax) to ensure paste completes
3. Alternative submission mechanism

## Impact

- Swarm of 10 workers had multiple stalls at startup
- Required manual tmux send-keys to unstick them
- Defeats purpose of automated spawning

## References

- Observed during Mad Max swarm (Dec 18, 2025)
- Affects gt spawn command in internal/cmd/spawn.go","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:06:50.734123-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T21:08:22.400899-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T21:08:22.400899-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-yd98","title":"Molecule format bridge: convert embedded markdown to child issues","description":"## Problem

Two molecule formats exist:
1. **Old (gastown builtin)**: Steps embedded as markdown in Description field, parsed by ParseMoleculeSteps()
2. **New (bd mol)**: Steps as child issues in proper beads DAG

The daemon's InstantiateMolecule() uses the old format. bd mol spawn uses the new format.

## Options

### Option A: Require child issues (new format)
- Update daemon to use bd mol spawn instead of InstantiateMolecule
- Deprecate embedded markdown format
- Pro: One format, simpler
- Con: Breaking change for existing molecules

### Option B: Build a bridge
- In InstantiateMolecule, detect format:
  - If Description has ## Step: markers → parse to child issues
  - If issue has children with template label → use directly
- Convert old format to new format on instantiation
- Pro: Backward compatible
- Con: More complexity

## Recommendation

Option B - build the bridge. On instantiation, convert markdown steps to child issues. This unifies execution while preserving authoring flexibility.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/valkyrie","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:55:15.751168-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:16:16.766608-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T01:16:16.766608-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main"}
